title: China-Brazil Economic Ties: Trade, Investment, and Opportunities
description: This article explores the economic ties between China and Brazil in trade and investment, highlighting potential investor opportunities.
extracted:
### Key Statistics on China-Brazil Bilateral Trade
- **2023 bilateral trade volume**: US$181.53 billion (6.1% year-on-year increase)
- **China’s exports to Brazil (2023)**: US$59.11 billion (4.3% year-on-year decrease)
- **China’s imports from Brazil (2023)**: US$122.42 billion (11.9% year-on-year increase)
- **Brazil’s trade surplus with China (2023)**: US$63.31 billion
- **Brazil as China’s largest source of agricultural imports (2023)**: Brazil exported agricultural products worth US$58.618 billion to China (over 50% of Brazil’s total trade with China; 24.85% of China’s overall agricultural imports)
- **China soybean imports from Brazil (2023)**: 69.95 million tons (29% year-on-year increase)
- **China corn imports from Brazil (2023)**: 12.81 million tons (47% of China’s total corn imports)
- **Brazilian beef exports to China (2023)**: 54.9% of Brazil’s total beef exports
- **Number of Brazilian meat companies authorized to export to China (2024 update)**: Increased from 106 to 144
- **China’s coffee market size (2023)**: RMB 265.4 billion (US$36.5 billion); average annual compound growth rate of 17.14% over past three years
- **China’s coffee market size projection (end of 2024)**: RMB 313.3 billion (US$43.1 billion)
- **Brazil coffee exports to China (2022)**: 422,000 bags
- **Brazil coffee exports to China (2023)**: 1.5 million bags
- **Brazil coffee exports to China projection (2024)**: 2.5 million bags (65% increase from 2023); total export value US$525 million (RMB 3.722 billion)
- **China’s exports to Brazil (Q1 2023)**: RMB 114.28 billion (approx. US$16.1 billion; 25.7% increase)
- **China’s passenger car exports to Brazil (first four months of 2024)**: US$762 million (372.4% growth)
- **Chinese electric vehicle sales in Brazil (first four months of 2024)**: 48,000 units (8 times the number from same period last year); 36.2% of Brazil’s total imported electric vehicles
- **China as Brazil’s largest trading partner**: For 14 consecutive years
- **Brazil as first LatAm country to exceed US$100 billion in annual trade with China**: Yes
- **Brazil ranks as China’s ninth-largest trading partner**: Yes

### Brazil’s Top Exports to China (2023)
| Category | Value (US$ billion) | Annual growth in value (2019-2023) | Share in Brazil’s exports (%) |
|----------|---------------------|------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Oil seeds and oleaginous fruits; miscellaneous grains, seeds, and fruit | 38.9 | 19 | 72 |
| Ores, slag and ash | 20.7 | 8 | 59 |
| Mineral fuels, mineral oils and products of their distillation; bituminous substances; mineral waxes | 20.1 | 10 | 36 |
| Meat and edible meat offal | 8.3 | 18 | 38 |
| Pulp of wood or of other fibrous cellulosic material; recovered (waste and scrap) paper or paperboard | 3.8 | 4 | 48 |
| Cereals | 3.6 | 399 | 24 |
| Sugars and sugar confectionery | 1.9 | 42 | 12 |
| Cotton | 1.5 | 14 | 47 |

**Source**: ICT Trade Map

**Additional Brazil export details to China (2023)**:
- Main agricultural products: soybeans, corn, beef, meat products, sugar, coffee beans
- Brazil as China’s largest supplier: soybeans, chicken, sugar

### China’s Top Exports to Brazil (2023)
| Category | Value (US$ billion) | Annual growth in value (2019-2023) | Share in Brazil’s exports (%) |
|----------|---------------------|------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Electrical machinery and equipment and parts thereof; sound recorders and reproducers, television | 14.3 | 15 | 2 |
| Nuclear reactors, boilers, machinery, and mechanical appliances; parts thereof | 9.0 | 19 | 2 |
| Vehicles other than railway or tramway rolling stock, and parts and accessories thereof | 4.1 | 32 | 2 |
| Organic chemicals | 3.6 | 17 | 5 |
| Plastics and articles thereof | 2.3 | 21 | 2 |
| Iron and steel | 2.3 | 45 | 3 |
| Miscellaneous chemical products | 1.7 | 37 | 4 |
| Fertilizers | 1.6 | 37 | 16 |

**Source**: ICT Trade Map

**Additional China export details to Brazil**:
- Primary exports: machinery, computer and communication technology equipment, instruments, textiles, steel, transportation equipment
- Significant growth potential: new energy products, electric and hybrid vehicles, high-tech products
